data.rationale: WINDOW signals: 1; EPISODE signals: 1; RECURRING signals: 1
WINDOW signals: 1; EPISODE signals: 1; RECURRING signals: 2
WINDOW signals: 2; SINGLE signals: 1; RECURRING signals: 1
The phrase 'required at screening' indicates a one-time assessment performed at the beginning of the study.
Collection 'beyond Cycle 7' and 'from Cycle 7 and on' implies repeated collection at scheduled intervals or visits after Cycle 7.
The instruction 'prior to administration of PF-06863135 on that day' suggests that these samples are collected repeatedly before each recurring treatment administration.
The assessment is triggered by specific conditions (e.g., 'Grade ≥3 CRS'), indicating a conditional workflow with decision points.

data.text: For thrombocytopenia: dosing can continue if platelets ≥25 × 109/L. For Grade 4 (first occurrence), resume at same dose level when platelets ≥25 × 109/L; for Grade 4 (second occurrence), resumption at a reduced dose level is recommended when platelets ≥25 × 109/L.

data.text: STUDY ASSESSMENTS AND PROCEDURES The investigator (or an appropriate delegate at the investigator site) must obtain a signed and dated ICD before performing any study-specific procedures. Study procedures and their timing are summarized in the SoA. Protocol waivers or exemptions are not allowed. Safety issues should be discussed with the sponsor immediately upon occurrence or awareness to determine whether the participant should continue or discontinue study intervention. Adherence to the study design requirements, including those specified in the SoA, is essential and required for study conduct. All screening evaluations must be completed and reviewed to confirm that potential participants meet all eligibility criteria. The investigator will maintain a screening log to CCI
data.sourceText: STUDY ASSESSMENTS AND PROCEDURES The investigator (or an appropriate delegate at the investigator si

data.text: Contact the medical monitor within 24 hours.
data.sourceText: Contact the medical monitor within 24 hours.

data.text: Closely monitor the participant for any AEs/SAEs, including signs of CRS, and laboratory abnormalities for at least 28 days after the overdose.
data.sourceText: Closely monitor the participant for any AEs/SAEs, including signs of CRS, and laboratory abnormaliti

data.text: Document the quantity of the excess dose as well as the duration of the overdose in the CRF.
data.sourceText: Document the quantity of the excess dose as well as the duration of the overdose in the CRF.

data.text: Overdose is reportable to Safety only when associated with an SAE.
data.sourceText: Overdose is reportable to Safety only when associated with an SAE.

data.text: Obtain a plasma sample for PK analysis within 28 days from the date of the last dose of elranatamab if requested by the medical monitor (determined on a case by case basis). Decisions regarding dose interruptions or modifications will be made by the investigator in consultation with the medical monitor based on the clinical evaluation of the participant.
